author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
# Findings

`jjf stale [--days N] [--status S] [--label L...] [--type T...] [--limit N] [--json]` ships.

- **Storage**: `Storage::stale(threshold_secs) -> Vec<StaleHit>` reads
  the snapshot cache, computes `now - updated_at` against the env-
  pinned clock (`JJF_TEST_CLOCK_SECS`), returns stale rows sorted
  ascending by `updated_at`. Strict `>` boundary semantics (touched
  exactly at the threshold tick is NOT stale). Future-dated stamps
  are guarded by `saturating_sub` (never stale).
- **CLI**: Defaults are `--days 14`, `--status open`, `--limit 0`
  (unlimited). JSON output is a bare array (mirrors `ls --json`,
  distinct from `search`'s envelope). Plain text columns:
  `<id>\t<age>\t<title>\t<status>`, silent on empty.
- **Age rendering**: single-token, never compound. `< 30d` -> `Nd`;
  `30-89d` -> `Nw`; `>= 90d` -> `Nmo`. Boundaries chosen so the
  common 2-4 week stale-set stays in `Nd` form (`19d` reads faster
  than `2w` at a glance).
- **Helpers added**: `rfc3339_to_epoch_secs` + `days_from_civil`
  (inverse of the existing `civil_from_days`) — round-trip on the
  emitted second-resolution stamps without bringing in `chrono` /
  `time`.
- **Tests**: 11 storage + 12 CLI integration. Cover boundary,
  oldest-first sort, future-dated guard, status/label/type filter
  composition, `--limit` truncation, age rendering at each
  boundary, JSON-vs-plain shape, empty-result behavior.
- **Smoke** (against jjforge's own planner): `--days 7 --limit 10`
  returns empty (heavy recent activity); `--days 1 --label
  epic:host-asterinas --json` returns 3 issues including
  `e726cde` itself; `--days 999` returns empty with exit 0.
- **Workspace**: 579 tests pass (was 556; +23 from this ticket).
- **Commit**: `4dc1a2ee`. Files: `crates/jjf-storage/src/lib.rs`,
  `crates/jjf/src/main.rs`, `crates/jjf-storage/tests/stale.rs`,
  `crates/jjf/tests/stale.rs`, `docs/cli-json.md`, `CLAUDE.md`.

# Recommendation

Close. Both asterinas-audit primitives (`search` + `stale`) are now
shipped; `host-asterinas-migrate` (`cc2fa96`) is ready to plan.

# Confidence

High. Storage layer is unit-tested at the boundary; CLI is driven
end-to-end against compiled binary with clock-pinned subprocesses
under nextest (each test in its own process so the env var doesn't
leak). Smoke-validated against the planner itself.

# Open follow-ups

- **`updated_at` semantics drift**: the ticket's "Out of scope"
  caveat says "comments don't bump `updated_at` today (only
  mutating verbs do)", but reading `Storage::add_comment_once` at
  `crates/jjf-storage/src/lib.rs:3041` shows
  `record.updated_at = now_rfc3339()?` — comments DO bump it.
  The storage-test
  `stale_comment_bumps_updated_at_today` snapshots the observed
  behavior so a future change to `add_comment` trips an alarm.
  Reconciling the spec (which behavior is correct?) is a separate
  decision — file as a storage-format ticket. If "comments don't
  bump" is the right contract, `add_comment` needs to stop
  touching `updated_at` (and `stale` behavior shifts accordingly).
  If "comments DO bump" is intentional, the storage spec needs
  the doc update.
- **Age-format boundaries**: I picked `< 30d / 30-89d / >= 90d`
  rather than the dispatch's suggested `< 14d / 14-59d / >= 60d`
  because the dispatch explicitly required `19d -> "19d"` which
  contradicts its own `>= 14d -> Nw` rule. The 30/90 split
  keeps the common stale-set in `Nd` form. Operator preference;
  trivially adjustable.
- **`unblocked-by` / activity-aware variants**: tracked elsewhere
  in CLAUDE.md's "filters jjforge doesn't yet ship" list. Not
  blockers for `host-asterinas-migrate`.
